---
title: "Interactive World Map"
---

# Interactive Prototype

This page embeds the interactive visualization prototype directly into the
documentation website. It is the same map as the standalone Flask app
([`src/visualize_master_data.py`](../src/visualize_master_data.py)), but
rebuilt as a self-contained Plotly figure so it works as a **static,
deployable HTML page** with no server.

Use the dropdown in the top-left of the map to switch between the
pixel-level aridity heatmap and the three country-level choropleth metrics.
Hover over any country (or grid point) for details.

```{python}
#| label: fig-interactive-map
#| fig-cap: "Global aridity, population density and GDP per capita. Switch metrics via the dropdown; hover for per-country detail."
#| echo: true
#| code-fold: true

from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go

# --- Locate output_data/ regardless of Quarto's execute directory ----------
ROOT = Path.cwd()
while not (ROOT / "output_data").exists() and ROOT != ROOT.parent:
    ROOT = ROOT.parent

MASTER_CSV = ROOT / "output_data" / "aggregated_master_data.csv"
ARIDITY_GRID_CSV = ROOT / "output_data" / "aridity_grid.csv"

df = pd.read_csv(MASTER_CSV)
grid = pd.read_csv(ARIDITY_GRID_CSV)

# Drop ocean / no-data points: real land aridity is always > 0, only
# ocean and no-data pixels sit at exactly 0 and would flood the map.
grid = grid[grid["aridity_index"] > 0]

# --- Pastel palette (shared with the docs theme) ---------------------------
PAPER = "#cacaca"
INK = "#2f3142"
INK_SOFT = "#6a6c7d"
LINE = "#e0dcef"

# Map base: clear separation between water and landmass
OCEAN = "#bccadf"   # distinct slate-blue so water is visible
LAND = "#f4f1ea"    # neutral warm land so pastel pixels stand out
COAST = "#8f9cb3"   # defined coastline for a crisp land/ocean edge

ARIDITY_SCALE = [[0.0, "#f6e6c5"], [0.5, "#9bd0c0"], [1.0, "#7fa8d9"]]
DENSITY_SCALE = [[0.0, "#fbede4"], [0.5, "#efb59c"], [1.0, "#df8fa6"]]
GDP_SCALE = [[0.0, "#eef5ec"], [0.5, "#a9d8c2"], [1.0, "#7fc8b0"]]

# --- Shared light geo layout ----------------------------------------------
GEO_LAYOUT = dict(
    showframe=False,
    showcoastlines=True,
    coastlinecolor=COAST,
    showland=True,
    landcolor=LAND,
    showocean=True,
    oceancolor=OCEAN,
    showcountries=True,
    countrycolor="#d8d2c4",
    showlakes=True,
    lakecolor=OCEAN,
    projection=dict(type="natural earth"),
    bgcolor=OCEAN,
)

HOVERLABEL = dict(
    bgcolor="#ffffff",
    bordercolor="#8b80d6",
    font=dict(family="Inter, sans-serif", size=13, color=INK),
)

# Country-level metrics: (column, label, colorscale, zmin, zmax)
METRICS = [
    ("aridity_index", "Aridity Index (avg)", ARIDITY_SCALE, 0, 2.5),
    ("population_density_2022", "Pop. Density (ppl/km²)", DENSITY_SCALE, 0, 500),
    ("gdp_per_capita_2024", "GDP per Capita (USD)", GDP_SCALE, 0, 100000),
]

# Per-country hover text (matches buildHoverText in the Flask app)
customdata = df[
    [
        "country_name", "country_code", "region", "income_group",
        "aridity_index", "population_density_2022", "gdp_per_capita_2024",
    ]
].values

HOVERTEMPLATE = (
    "<b>%{customdata[0]}</b><br>"
    "─────────────────────<br>"
    "<b>Code:</b> %{customdata[1]}<br>"
    "<b>Region:</b> %{customdata[2]}<br>"
    "<b>Income:</b> %{customdata[3]}<br>"
    "─────────────────────<br>"
    "🌡️ <b>Aridity:</b> %{customdata[4]:.4f}<br>"
    "👥 <b>Pop. Density:</b> %{customdata[5]:,.2f} /km²<br>"
    "💰 <b>GDP/cap:</b> $%{customdata[6]:,.0f}"
    "<extra></extra>"
)

fig = go.Figure()

# --- Trace 0: pixel-level aridity heatmap ----------------------------------
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scattergeo(
        lat=grid["lat"],
        lon=grid["lon"],
        mode="markers",
        marker=dict(
            size=4,
            opacity=0.7,
            color=grid["aridity_index"],
            colorscale=ARIDITY_SCALE,
            cmin=0,
            cmax=2.5,
            colorbar=dict(
                title=dict(text="Aridity Index",
                           font=dict(color=INK_SOFT, size=12)),
                tickfont=dict(color=INK_SOFT),
                bgcolor="#ffffff",
                bordercolor=LINE,
                outlinecolor=LINE,
                len=0.6,
            ),
            line=dict(width=0),
        ),
        hovertemplate=(
            "<b>Aridity Index</b>: %{marker.color:.4f}"
            "<br>Lat: %{lat:.2f}°  Lon: %{lon:.2f}°<extra></extra>"
        ),
        hoverlabel=HOVERLABEL,
        name="Aridity (heatmap)",
        visible=True,
    )
)

# --- Traces 1-3: country-level choropleths ---------------------------------
for col, label, scale, zmin, zmax in METRICS:
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Choropleth(
            locations=df["country_code"],
            z=df[col],
            zmin=zmin,
            zmax=zmax,
            customdata=customdata,
            hovertemplate=HOVERTEMPLATE,
            hoverlabel={**HOVERLABEL, "align": "left"},
            colorscale=scale,
            colorbar=dict(
                title=dict(text=label, font=dict(color=INK_SOFT, size=12)),
                tickfont=dict(color=INK_SOFT),
                bgcolor="#ffffff",
                bordercolor=LINE,
                outlinecolor=LINE,
                len=0.6,
            ),
            marker_line=dict(color="#cfcadf", width=0.5),
            name=label,
            visible=False,
        )
    )

# --- Metric switcher (replaces the HTML <select>) --------------------------
buttons = [
    dict(label="Aridity Index (Heatmap)", method="update",
         args=[{"visible": [True, False, False, False]}]),
    dict(label="Aridity Index (Country avg)", method="update",
         args=[{"visible": [False, True, False, False]}]),
    dict(label="Population Density (2022)", method="update",
         args=[{"visible": [False, False, True, False]}]),
    dict(label="GDP per Capita (2024)", method="update",
         args=[{"visible": [False, False, False, True]}]),
]

fig.update_layout(
    geo=GEO_LAYOUT,
    paper_bgcolor=PAPER,
    plot_bgcolor=PAPER,
    font=dict(family="Inter, sans-serif", color=INK),
    margin=dict(l=0, r=0, t=40, b=0),
    height=650,
    updatemenus=[
        dict(
            buttons=buttons,
            direction="down",
            x=0.01,
            xanchor="left",
            y=0.99,
            yanchor="top",
            bgcolor="#ffffff",
            bordercolor=LINE,
            font=dict(color=INK, size=12),
            showactive=True,
        )
    ],
)

fig.show()
```
